ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 220, DEFENDANT RAYTHEON COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT RAYTHEON COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS NOT SATISFIED ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION THAT PLAINTIFF DOES NOT POSSESS AND CANNOT REASONABLY OBTAIN EVIDENCE THAT HE WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. BY FAILING TO PROPOUND CONTENTION DISCOVERY, DEFENDANT DID NOT SATISFY ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION. DEFENDANT'S FAILURE TO PRESENT PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSES TO CONTENTION DISCOVERY PRECLUDES DEFENDANT'S RELIANCE ON THE FACTUALLY DEVOID PRONG OF AGUILAR. MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Daniel M. Crowley, Esq. of BOOTH, MITCHEL & STRANGE LLP (213-738-0100), appearing for defendant / moving party Rytheon Company
Gilbert L. Purcell, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Lee Nixt

RULING:
DEFENDANT RAYTHEON COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Raytheon Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant has not satisfied its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos attributable to defendant. By failing to propound contention discovery, defendant did not satisfy its burden of production. Defendant's failure to present plaintiff's responses to contention discovery precludes defendant's reliance on the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff's objection to the declaration of Jaime Fraser are sustained. Order signed in open court.Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
